title: Tennis Star Carlos Alcaraz Will Miss the French Open Due to Injury
summary:
Bad news for tennis fans! Carlos Alcaraz, the two-time defending champion, will not play in the French Open next month. The 22-year-old tennis star injured his right wrist at the Barcelona Open last week. He had tests, and the results showed he needs to be careful. So he decided to skip both the Italian Open and the French Open.

Alcaraz is one of the best tennis players in the world. He has won seven Grand Slam titles (the biggest tournaments in tennis). Earlier this year, he won the Australian Open. He became the youngest man ever to win all four Grand Slams at least once. That's called a career Grand Slam.

His big rival, Jannik Sinner, is now ranked number one. Sinner said he was sad to hear about Alcaraz's injury. "It is very sad news," Sinner said. "Hopefully he can be back for Wimbledon." Wimbledon is another big tournament that starts in July.

Alcaraz posted on Instagram: "This is a difficult time for me, but I am sure we will come out of this stronger." Fans are hoping to see him back on the court soon.
why_it_matters: This shows that even top athletes get injured, and it reminds us to take care of our bodies and not push too hard when we are hurt.

keywords.explanation: The player who won the tournament the last time and is trying to win again.
One of the four biggest tennis tournaments, played in Paris on clay courts.
The joint between your hand and your arm that can be injured.
One of the four most important tennis tournaments in the world: Australian Open, French Open, Wimbledon, and US Open.
Winning all four Grand Slam tournaments at least once in your life.
A competitor or opponent that you often play against and try to beat.

background_read: The French Open is one of the four 'Grand Slam' tennis tournaments, and it is played every spring in Paris, France.
Carlos Alcaraz is from Spain and is one of the best young tennis players ever.
Jannik Sinner (a top tennis player from Italy) took over the number one ranking after Alcaraz's injury.
Article_Structure: WHO: Carlos Alcaraz (tennis star), Jannik Sinner (his rival), and tennis fans.
WHAT: Alcaraz injured his wrist and will miss the French Open and Italian Open.
WHERE: At the French Open tournament (and also the Barcelona Open where he got hurt).
WHY: Because he hurt his right wrist and needs time to heal carefully.

perspectives.description: Fans are disappointed because they wanted to see Alcaraz play at the French Open, but they hope he recovers soon.
Alcaraz is sad but says he is sure he will come back stronger after resting and healing.
Sinner said it is very sad news and he hopes Alcaraz can come back for Wimbledon.
